recentResults.highlight: CSK do the double over MI — Ruturaj Gaikwad PoM (anchor 60+), Kartik Sharma seals it with a reverse lap; Naman Dhir 57(37); Dhoni STILL OUT (calf), CSK climb to 6th
Raghuvanshi fifty + Varun-Narine spin magic seal 7-wicket win; SRH 5-match streak ENDS, KKR's 3rd straight win, climb to 8th
GT chase 164 — PBKS 2nd loss in a row, lose top spot tightening grip; GT to 5th with 12 points after 3rd straight win
MI chase 229 — first win in 5; LSG hit 7th loss, stay bottom of table (4 pts, NRR -1.076)
CSK back-to-back 8-wkt wins! DC's 3rd loss in 4; CSK climb to 6th at 10 pts, suddenly in playoff math; DC slip to 7th with NRR -0.949

upcomingFixtures.venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow

storylines.title: CSK do the double over MI — back-to-back 8-wicket wins resurrect playoff hopes
storylines.body: Chennai Super Kings have suddenly come alive at the right time. After their 8-wicket demolition of MI at Chepauk on May 2 (Gaikwad PoM with a composed 60+, Kartik Sharma sealing it with a reverse lap), they followed up with another 8-wicket win — this time over DC at Arun Jaitley on May 5. Two convincing wins in three days have lifted CSK from the brink to 6th with 10 points and a +0.151 NRR — back in the playoff conversation when they had been written off two weeks ago. Sanju Samson is now in the Orange Cap top 5 with 402 runs at AVG 57.43. The Dhoni question still lingers (calf, hasn't played a match all season), but the team has rediscovered its rhythm without him. With 4 matches left, CSK need probably 3 wins and big NRR boosts — but for the first time in IPL 2026, that math feels possible.

storylines.title: KL Rahul takes Orange Cap with 445 runs — DC opener leads despite team chaos
storylines.body: KL Rahul has wrested the Orange Cap from Abhishek Sharma. After 10 matches and 10 innings, Rahul leads with 445 runs at AVG 49.44, SR 180.89, with 44 fours and 24 sixes. The remarkable part: he's done this for a team that's slipped to 7th with 4-6 record and -0.949 NRR. His 152 vs PBKS in the T20 record chase remains his peak, but his consistency (rarely dismissed cheap) has made him IPL 2026's most reliable batter. Abhishek Sharma sits 2nd on 440 (just 5 behind), Klaasen 3rd on 425, Sooryavanshi 4th on 404, and Sanju Samson sneaks into 5th with 402 — pushing Kohli (379) and Gill (373) out of the top 5. Five players over 400 runs in IPL 2026 — a tournament first.

storylines.title: Bhuvi and Kamboj joint #1 on 17 wickets — Purple Cap race tightens with three teams in playoff push
storylines.body: Bhuvneshwar Kumar (RCB, economy 7.54) and Anshul Kamboj (CSK) are now level on 17 wickets each at the top of the Purple Cap. Kagiso Rabada (GT, 16) jumped into 3rd, while Archer (15) and Malinga (15) round out the top 5. The race is now genuinely wide open with all five players from playoff-contention sides. Bhuvi's economy is the standout — at 7.54 he's the only top-5 bowler under 8 RPO. Kamboj's spell yesterday vs DC takes him level. Going into the final week of the league, this is now a pace bowler's tournament after weeks of being dominated by batters scoring 220+ totals.

storylines.title: PBKS lose 2 in a row — top spot in jeopardy as 5 teams jump within 3 points
storylines.body: Punjab Kings, who looked invincible at 6-0-1 in mid-April, have now lost back-to-back matches: first to RR at Mullanpur (chasing 222), then to GT at Ahmedabad (failing to defend 163). They cling to top spot only by virtue of the rain abandonment vs KKR — their NRR has crashed from +1.333 to +0.855. With 5 league games left, the top 5 (PBKS, RCB, SRH, RR, GT) are all within 1 point of each other. Iyer's side still have a great shot at top 2 but the wobble is real. Their bowling — always the weak point — has been exposed in two straight chases. The unbeaten run that defined their April has become a memory.

storylines.title: DC's whiplash season — 264 chase, then 75 all out, then 226 chase, then 75 all out vs CSK
storylines.body: Delhi Capitals have produced perhaps the most volatile season in IPL history. In the span of two weeks: chased 265 (highest ever T20 chase), got bowled out for 75 by RCB, chased 226 in 19.1 vs RR (impressive heist at Jaipur), then collapsed to 155/7 against CSK at home. Across 10 matches they've moved from 5th to 7th and back again repeatedly. KL Rahul tops the Orange Cap personally, but the team can't put two consistent games together. Axar Patel's captaincy has come under scrutiny — and with NRR at -0.949, even mathematical playoff hopes look bleak. They have 4 league games left, but realistically need to win all 4 with big margins to give themselves a chance. The team is genuinely confusing — capable of brilliance, capable of total collapse, often within 48 hours.

storylines.title: Dhoni watch continues — calf injury keeps CSK legend out of El Clasico, mentor role looking likely
storylines.body: Even with CSK winning back-to-back, MS Dhoni still hasn't played a single match in IPL 2026. The El Clasico against MI on May 2 was widely expected to be his return — instead, Ruturaj confirmed at the toss he was still not fit. Batting coach Michael Hussey said Dhoni was 'nearing return' and working specifically on running between wickets, but the calf strain has lingered for nearly 8 weeks now. CSK's resurgence without him — Gaikwad anchoring, Samson firing, Brevis showing flashes — has shifted the narrative. The team that needed Dhoni to win games no longer does. With 4 league games left, the window to see him bat one final time at Chepauk is closing fast. The retirement announcement increasingly looks like it'll come quietly in the offseason rather than via a final innings. A bittersweet end if the season finishes without him in whites.
